raw_source_data.title: TechOps Architect
raw_source_data.raw_description: We’re really excited to be building a small, thoughtful and effective TechOps team at Octopus. We’re looking for a knowledgeable and experienced TechOps Architect to help shape how we support people across the business - someone who can lead with clarity, build with empathy, and bring a mix of technical depth and sound judgment. There’s loads to get stuck into, from identity management and scripting to device management and scalable tooling, areas where most of our work sits and where we need thoughtful, sustainable solutions. You’ll play an important role in shaping how we work: setting direction without overcomplicating things, supporting the team with care, and balancing technical curiosity with a down-to-earth mindset. We’re after someone who can spot where things could be better, help others grow, and make improvements that stick. If you’re excited about solving meaningful problems and shaping how TechOps supports a growing, people-first business – we’d love to chat. What is TechOps? A more modern, more lean version of a traditional enterprise IT team. We cover everything from what most people would call “1st line support” all the way up to high-level strategic planning, and everything in between. We focus on advising rather than enforcing, and give people the autonomy to work with technology to solve their problems in the way that works best for them. We’re an autonomous team who look after a wide range of technology across the business. We empower teams to do the best work they can whilst keeping things running efficiently in the background. We tackle big problems and the breadth of work is huge. Dealing with ambiguity is a key part of working in TechOps, and every day will be a new and exciting one, working on varying challenges across the company. We have a cloud-first approach to everything we do. This allows us to scale quickly and focus on things where we can add the most value. No on-prem AD here! About the role As a TechOps Architect, You will be responsible for high-level strategic and architectural technical decisions around the business. We also strongly believe that no matter what role you have within TechOps, it’s still important to keep your ear to the ground and see, first hand, the problems our users are facing, and the impact that decisions you make have on everyone at Octopus. This means you’ll spend some of your time helping out with -day “1st, 2nd and 3rd line” support. You’ll have a wide breadth of knowledge and experience across all areas of IT, but also an immensely deep understanding of many of those areas too. You’ll likely be an acknowledged expert among your peers in Identity and Access Management, and have a passion for hacking and building. You will have interest and/or experience in related areas like software engineering, security, platform architecture, UI/UX design etc. Roles that you might currently be in: IAM Engineer, Tech Lead, Lead/Senior Support Engineer, Site Reliability Engineer, Systems Architect, Systems Designer, Senior Systems Engineer, Workplace Technology Architect, Solutions Architect We’re looking for a knowledgeable and experienced to help shape how we support people across the business - someone who can lead with clarity, build with empathy, and bring a mix of technical depth and sound judgment. If you’re excited about solving meaningful problems and shaping how TechOps supports a growing, people-first business – we’d love to chat. raw_source_data.title: EV Sales Specialist (B2C)
raw_source_data.raw_description: We're looking for an Electric Vehicles Sales Specialist, who will be responsible for managing sales directly with our customers focusing on our salary sacrifice product. You will be the face and the voice of the Octopus Electric Vehicles, advising and supporting customers throughout their journey towards purchasing an EV. This role will include an uncapped commission scheme and a generous car allowance as part of the overall package and will be based in our brand new office Weybridge 3 days a week You will discuss their and find the perfect vehicle for their lifestyle, considering the salary sacrifice product fully and the of going electric. A genuine passion for customer experience and sustainability is a must with a strong understanding of process and the importance of customer service

raw_source_data.title: Manager des Equipes Techniques
raw_source_data.raw_description: 🐙 Rejoins la révolution énergétique chez Octopus Energy! Octopus Energy, fournisseur d’énergie verte présent à l’international, a lancé sa filiale Octopus Energy Services pour accompagner ses clients dans la décarbonation complète de leur foyer. Déjà bien implantée au Royaume-Uni, cette entité propose un ensemble de solutions techniques: installation de panneaux photovoltaïques, de bornes de recharge pour véhicules électriques et de systèmes de chauffage décarboné, le tout avec une approche centrée sur la qualité, l’innovation et sans sous-traitance. Tu veux un job qui a du sens? Octopus Energy Services a démarré son activité en 2024, avec un objectif clair: aider les particuliers à réduire leurs factures énergétiques tout en participant activement à la transition énergétique. Et aujourd’hui on a besoin de toi pour propulser notre croissance! Tes deux grandes missions

raw_source_data.title: Onboarding Executive
raw_source_data.raw_description: You will lead a team of onboarding executives who support a critical part of our customer journey, ensuring every employer has a world-class experience rolling out our EV salary sacrifice scheme to their employees. You will be responsible for defining and building the phase in our onboarding journey for companies of all sizes, from self-serve options for small businesses to complex bespoke projects for large enterprises. This is a player/manager role, so you will own a portfolio of projects and represent the onboarding team during the business development process, alongside your leadership responsibility for the team. A genuine passion for team leadership and customer experience is a must, alongside a collaborative and positive approach, and strong data analysis and project management skills. raw_source_data.title: Solar Field Technician – Rosamond, CA Remote
raw_source_data.function: is driving meaningful change for future generations
raw_source_data.raw_description: This position will report directly to the project Site Manager in Rosamond, CA. The Field Technician will be responsible for assigned aspects of the maintenance and operation of utility scale PV power plant. Duties include monitoring PV Plant operation through SCADA, commissioning, testing, inspecting, troubleshooting, repairing, and modifying AC and DC solar PV systems, including inverters, PV module arrays, transformers, switchgear, trackers, and metering systems. Additionally, this position is responsible for the supervision of contractors to ensure safe, high quality and timely performance of maintenance activities in the field

You will manage core administrative processes while also evaluating jobs, analyzing market information, and helping to develop process improvements that directly influence how we attract, retain, and reward talent. This position plays a critical role in ensuring our total rewards programs are competitive, equitable, and aligned with our environmental mission. , merit, bonus, promotions) through data validation, modelin Overall Function The is an early-career role supporting both and Compensation. raw_source_data.title: Creator Lead

raw_source_data.title: Manager, Membership Mobilization
raw_source_data.raw_description: The Membership Mobilization Manager works to advance GoodPower’s work to accelerate a renewable energy economy through building a membership engagement program across GoodPower and its independent sub-brands who are primed to take high level actions from attending and organizing events in their communities, mobilizing others, calling and writing to elected leaders, submitting public comments, testifying before government bodies, writing op-eds and letters to the editor, engaging in their communities, and sharing their stories. raw_source_data.description: Florida Power & Light Company is the largest electric utility in the U.S., providing reliable energy to nearly 12 million Floridians. With one of the nation’s most fuel-efficient, cost-effective power generation fleets and industry-leading reliability, we’re redefining what’s possible in energy. Want to be part of something powerful? Join our outstanding team and help shape the future of energy.

raw_source_data.description: Learn more about our open positions and how you can join the ACORE team. About A career at ACORE offers a unique opportunity to be at the forefront of the clean energy transition, working on the cross-cutting clean energy business and policy issues that support an improved quality of life for all. Internship applications are welcome from rising seniors, new graduates, and graduate students committed to making clean energy the focus of their future career. Careers ACORE is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit based in Washington, D. Award-Winning ACORE is proud to be named by The NonProfit Times as one of the best nonprofits to work for in 2023.
raw_source_data.notes: Scraped from https://acore.org/about/careers/

raw_source_data.title: Senior Manager, California State Affairs
raw_source_data.raw_description: The Senior Manager, California State Affairs will lead the development and implementation of key aspects of EDF’s California-based work. Reporting to Senior Director, California, the Senior Manager’s responsibilities will include leading the collaborative design and implementation of effective education and policy advocacy campaigns to support EDF’s major workstream priorities in California. This work will entail managing consultants, coordinating among multiple EDF workstreams and experts, coordinating with partner organizations, owning relationships with key stakeholders, influential decisionmakers and/or government agencies, and representing EDF and organizational positions to external stakeholders. The Senior Manager will be a key “on the ground” presence for EDF and EDF Action in Sacramento, representing the organization and its priorities. raw_source_data.title: Project Development Analyst
raw_source_data.raw_description: The Project Development Analyst is an exciting early‑career role at the forefront of the utility‑scale renewable energy industry, supporting the development of wind, solar, and battery storage projects across their full lifecycle. This fast‑paced, collaborative position is embedded within project teams and works closely with experienced development, engineering, legal, finance, and real estate professionals. Analysts support projects from early‑stage land acquisition and stakeholder engagement through permitting, interconnection, and preparation for construction. This role is well‑suited for highly motivated, detail‑oriented individuals seeking hands‑on responsibility, exposure to complex infrastructure projects, and strong professional growth opportunities in renewable energy. raw_source_data.title: Manager, Market & Asset Operations
raw_source_data.raw_description: The Manager, Market & Asset Operations owns Fervo's day-ahead market interface in CAISO and is accountable for the quality and financial performance of geothermal assets from bid execution through handoff to real-time operations. You will serve as the primary point of contact for the Scheduling Coordinator relationship, coordinating closely with Plant Operations, Process Engineering, Interconnection partners, the customers, and internal Asset Management stakeholders to ensure market participation accurately reflects plant conditions, outage plans, and operating constraints at all times. The Manager, Market & Asset Operations owns Fervo's day-ahead market interface in CAISO and is accountable for the quality and financial performance of geothermal assets from bid execution through handoff to real-time operations. You will serve as the primary point of contact for the Scheduling Coordinator relationship, coordinating closely with Plant Operations, Process Engineering, Interconnection partners, the customers, and internal Asset Management stakeholders to ensure market participation accurately reflects plant conditions, outage plans, and operating constraints at all times You will serve as the primary point raw_source_data.description: The Manager, Market & Asset Operations owns Fervo's day-ahead market interface in CAISO and is accountable for the quality and financial performance of geothermal assets from bid execution through handoff to real-time operations. You will serve as the primary point of contact for the Scheduling Coordinator relationship, coordinating closely with Plant Operations, Process Engineering, Interconnection partners, the customers, and internal Asset Management stakeholders to ensure market participation accurately reflects plant conditions, outage plans, and operating constraints at all times.
raw_source_data.notes: Scraped from https://jobs.elementalimpact.com/jobs

display.title: Manager, Market & Asset Operations
display.description: The Manager, Market & Asset Operations owns Fervo's day-ahead market interface in CAISO and is accountable for the quality and financial performance of geothermal assets from bid execution through handoff to real-time operations. You will serve as the primary point of contact for the Scheduling Coordinator relationship, coordinating closely with Plant Operations, Process Engineering, Interconnection partners, the customers, and internal Asset Management stakeholders to ensure market participation accurately reflects plant conditions, outage plans, and operating constraints at all times.

admin_notes: organization corrected from Elemental Impact source board

raw_source_data.title: Senior Analyst, Asset & Portfolio Optimization
raw_source_data.raw_description: The Senior Analyst, Asset & Portfolio Optimization owns the financial performance analytics for Fervo's operating and contracted assets. You will build and maintain the models, forecasts, and dashboards that connect asset performance, market prices, operating constraints, and contract terms into a clear view of P&L — and translate that view into actionable recommendations for Asset Management, Finance, and commercial leadership. The Senior Analyst, Asset & Portfolio Optimization owns the financial performance analytics for Fervo's operating and contracted assets. raw_source_data.description: The Senior Analyst, Asset & Portfolio Optimization owns the financial performance analytics for Fervo's operating and contracted assets. You will build and maintain the models, forecasts, and dashboards that connect asset performance, market prices, operating constraints, and contract terms into a clear view of P&L — and translate that view into actionable recommendations for Asset Management, Finance, and commercial leadership.
raw_source_data.notes: Scraped from https://jobs.elementalimpact.com/jobs

raw_source_data.title: Senior Quality Assurance Engineer
raw_source_data.raw_description: VIA is looking for a strategic Senior Quality Assurance Engineer to define and own our quality standards. This is not a traditional 'downstream' QA role, you will be responsible for building the systems that allow our engineers to integrate testing into their -day workflow. You will act as a force multiplier by leveraging AI-driven automation to replace manual processes and ensure our products meet rigorous security requirements. VIA is looking for a strategic Senior Quality Assurance Engineer to define and own our quality standards. You will act as a force multiplier by leveraging AI-driven automation to replace manual processes and ensure our products meet rigorous security requirements This is not a traditional 'downstream' QA role, you will be responsible for building the systems that allow our engineers to integrate testing into their -day workflow. raw_source_data.title: Sr. Supplier Quality Engineer
raw_source_data.raw_description: will own supplier development strategy, drive resolution of complex quality issues, and serve as the primary technical interface between Proterra Powered and its manufacturing partners across busbars, castings, adhesives, stampings/forming, machined components, injection-molded housings, and related module commodities. The Senior Supplier Quality Engineer is a critical leadership role responsible for ensuring the quality, reliability, and continuous improvement of the supplier base supporting EV battery module production. This individual will own supplier development strategy, drive resolution of complex quality issues, and serve as the primary technical interface between Proterra Powered and its manufacturing partners raw_source_data.title: Geologic Data Technician
raw_source_data.raw_description: Teverra LLC is seeking a highly skilled and innovative Hydraulic Fracturing Modeling Engineer to support its R&D and consulting activities focused on Enhanced Geothermal Systems (EGS). The ideal candidate will have a strong understanding of rock mechanics, fracture propagation, and stimulation design workflows, with the ability to apply oil and gas expertise to geothermal environments. Candidates must demonstrate experience in fracture modeling and exhibit a forward-thinking, problem-solving mindset

raw_source_data.title: National Policy & Advocacy Campaigner
raw_source_data.raw_description: Why you should join the Solar United Neighbors team Remote work Our HQ is in D., but most of our team is spread across the U. Read our mission and vision As a parent of young kids, SUN is a supportive and flexible workplace. Roger Horowitz, Vice President of Go Solar Programs and support We provide full-time staff with a competitive total compensation package, including: Employer-sponsored health insurance (84% coverage for employees and dependents) 15 vacation days and 23 wellness days per year Paid holiday on all federal holidays — plus the day after Thanksgiving, the week between Christmas and New Year’s Day off, and two additional days of your choice Parental, family care, self-care, volunteer, bereavement, and jury leave 401(k) retirement account with match up to 2% after 1 year $1,000 professional development budget $50/month stipend for phone and internet SUN welcomes candidates of diverse backgrounds and experience to apply, including people of color, women, LGBTQ+, people with disabilities, veterans, and those with a non-traditional education. If our mission and goals are the “what” of our work, our values are the “how

raw_source_data.title: Field Sales Consultant
raw_source_data.raw_description: The Field Sales Consultant self-generates sales leads and presents in-home presentations on our solar products and services for potential customers. Provide an industry leading customer sales experience from initial contact through installation and after the customer's system is turned on. Handle the pipeline of activity from prospect to install by following up with all leads and in-home consultations. Employees on our Freedom Time Off Plan are not limited to a set number of days that they are entitled to take off from work and instead have the freedom to manage how much time off they need!. raw_source_data.title: Portfolio ESG Analyst
raw_source_data.raw_description: This role is ideal for someone who thrives on data-led approaches to ESG and sustainable finance, and who wants to help shape the future of responsible investment in renewable energy. The will work closely with the Asset Management and Fund Management teams across our operational and construction-stage assets. You will help ensure ESG risks are well managed and that sustainability and impact goals are consistently advanced. This is a highly visible role with the opportunity to take ownership of ESG reporting for select funds and support stewardship and engagement activities with underlying portfolio companies and asset managers. Our ambition is to change the entire energy lifecycle and make every green electron matter - delivering better outcomes for customers, investors, and the planet, while accelerating the transition to net zero. Help us make a big green dent in the universe. We’re on a mission to drive the global renewable energy revolution, and we need brilliant people to help make it happen. OEGEN (Octopus Energy Generation) is one of Europe’s leading investors in renewable energy and energy transition, with a track record dating back to 2010. Our team brings deep expertise across investment, portfolio, and asset management. We are structured for growth and long-term ownership, managing over £7bn in assets spanning onshore wind, solar, energy transition infrastructure, and climate tech. We are committed to best-in-class responsible investment, offering like-minded investors the ability to deploy capital sustainably. Our ambition is to change the entire energy lifecycle and make every green electron matter - delivering better outcomes for customers, investors, and the planet, while accelerating the transition to net zero. About the role: We are seeking a passionate and analytical ESG professional to join our London-based team. This role is ideal for someone who thrives on data-led approaches to ESG and sustainable finance, and who wants to help shape the future of responsible investment in renewable energy. The Portfolio ESG Analyst will work closely with the Asset Management and Fund Management teams across our operational and construction-stage assets. raw_source_data.title: Senior Treasury Analyst
raw_source_data.raw_description: Join our global team dedicated to sustainability and green tech, where energy is good for the planet and your soul 💚 We embrace flat hierarchies and an open, agile culture that fosters innovation, flexibility, and creativity. As the Group Treasury Lead, you will be instrumental in overseeing liquidity, cash management, and treasury operations of the group from a strategic perspective. You will support the company’s capital strategy by forecasting and analysing cash flows, managing liquidity across the group entities, and executing investment and debt facility strategies. Since stepping into the renewables arena in 2010, we've made waves - becoming a top investor in onshore wind across mainland Europe and the largest backer of solar power in Europe. At Octopus Energy Generation (OEGEN), we're passionate about powering a sustainable future. As pioneers in the renewable energy space, we don’t just invest in utility-scale projects; we invest in people, communities, and a greener tomorrow. Since stepping into the renewables arena in 2010, we've made waves - becoming a top investor in onshore wind across mainland Europe and the largest backer of solar power in Europe. Today, we proudly manage over £7bn in renewable energy assets worldwide. Our portfolio includes over three hundred major energy-generating companies, from wind farms to solar power plants, with exciting new ventures in energy storage, e-fuels and beyond. As we continue to grow, we're looking for passionate individuals to join our diverse and dedicated team, helping us drive the future of renewable energy forward. Whilst we maintain a degree of flexibility, this position requires an average of 3 days in the office per week in London At Octopus Energy, we're all about clean, green energy with transparent pricing and a relentless commitment to customer experience. raw_source_data.title: EV Mobility Consultant
raw_source_data.raw_description: Your mission is essential: In this founding phase, your role is a dynamic hybrid with a clear focus on the B2C sector, while actively supporting our B2B operations. You are the essential interface between our corporate clients and their workforce: B2C Excellence (Primary Focus): You provide individual and passionate guidance to the employees of our partner companies. Your mission is to turn the advisory process into a true experience, helping them find the perfect EV for our Salary Sacrifice model. B2B Support: As we build our presence in Germany, you will also assist in the B2B sector, helping to acquire new companies and integrate them into our ecosystem. *Please note - applications will be reviewed by our UK Talent team, so please upload your CV in english*

raw_source_data.title: Director - Capital Partnerships
raw_source_data.raw_description: Position Summary HASI is seeking an individual with experience with debt product structuring, securitization, and institutional investors to join our team. This individual will work on the Investment Team as a key interface with a large network of institutional investors. The core of the role will focus on raising capital for both historic HASI securitization transactions and new senior debt opportunities. The Director will interface with certain capital partners (generally insurance companies, pension funds and banks) on a periodic cadence to understand market dynamics and what opportunities might be a fit. As potential transactions with capital partners advance, the Director will lead execution to closing in collaboration with other members of the Investment Team

display.title: Director - Capital Partnerships
display.description: Position Summary HASI is seeking an individual with experience with debt product structuring, securitization, and institutional investors to join our team. This individual will work on the Investment Team as a key interface with a large network of institutional investors. The core of the role will focus on raising capital for both historic HASI securitization transactions and new senior debt opportunities. The Director will interface with certain capital partners (generally insurance companies, pension funds and banks) on a periodic cadence to understand market dynamics and what opportunities might be a fit. As potential transactions with capital partners advance, the Director will lead execution to closing in collaboration with other members of the Investment Team.

raw_source_data.title: Senior Associate
raw_source_data.raw_description: With more than $16 billion in managed assets, our investments are diversified across multiple asset classes, including utility-scale solar, storage, and onshore wind; distributed solar and storage; RNG; and energy efficiency. We combine deep expertise in energy markets and financial structuring with long-standing programmatic client partnerships to deliver superior risk-adjusted returns and measurable environmental. raw_source_data.title: Senior Manager - Technical Accounting
raw_source_data.raw_description: With more than $16 billion in managed assets, our investments are diversified across multiple asset classes, including utility-scale solar, storage, and onshore wind; distributed solar and storage; RNG; and energy efficiency. We combine deep expertise in energy markets and financial structuring with long-standing programmatic client partnerships to deliver superior risk-adjusted returns and measurable environmental. Position Summary HASI is seeking a Senior Manager – Technical Accounting to join our accounting team in Annapolis, MD. This role will be responsible for evaluating the appropriate accounting treatment for our investments (equity investments, debt securities, financing receivables, real estate and securitizations) as well as the subsequent accounting for certain complex transactions including research and applying facts to judgmental areas of the accounting guidance. This role will also support quarterly close activities such as entries for Equity Method Investments, Current Expected Credit Loss (CECL) analysis, and footnote development We combine deep expertise in energy markets and financial structuring with long-standing programmatic client partnerships to deliver superior risk-adjusted returns and measurable environmental. display.title: Senior Manager - Technical Accounting
display.description: With more than $16 billion in managed assets, our investments are diversified across multiple asset classes, including utility-scale solar, storage, and onshore wind; distributed solar and storage; RNG; and energy efficiency. We combine deep expertise in energy markets and financial structuring with long-standing programmatic client partnerships to deliver superior risk-adjusted returns and measurable environmental. Position Summary HASI is seeking a Senior Manager – Technical Accounting to join our accounting team in Annapolis, MD. This role will be responsible for evaluating the appropriate accounting treatment for our investments (equity investments, debt securities, financing receivables, real estate and securitizations) as well as the subsequent accounting for certain complex transactions including research and applying facts to judgmental areas of the accounting guidance. This role will also support quarterly close activities such as entries for Equity Method Investments, Current Expected Credit Loss (CECL) analysis, and footnote development.

raw_source_data.title: CRO Specialist
raw_source_data.raw_description: 🌍 Revolucionamos la energía para cambiar el mundo En Octopus Energy, no somos una eléctrica tradicional. Somos un equipo de personas apasionadas que trabaja cada día para hacer que la energía sea 100% renovable, accesible y justa para todos. Creemos en la tecnología y la innovación como motores del cambio. Queremos transformar la forma en que el mundo consume energía, eliminando barreras, simplificando procesos y ofreciendo un servicio más humano y transparente. Por esto mismo, nuestro compromiso es claro: la energía limpia debe ser la norma, no la excepción. Para lograrlo, combinamos sostenibilidad con un enfoque centrado en las personas, impulsando un servicio excepcional y construyendo un equipo increíble que comparte nuestra visión. 💡 Pero nada de esto sería posible sin personas como tú. Para seguir creciendo y transformando el mercado, buscamos talento con ganas de marcar la diferencia. Si quieres formar parte de este cambio, aportar tu energía y ayudar a construir un futuro más verde. ¿Qué estamos buscando? En Octopus Energy buscamos un/a CRO Specialist que entienda la conversión como lo que realmente es: una consecuencia de una propuesta de valor potente, una experiencia coherente y una ejecución impecable en todos los puntos de contacto. Este rol trata de entender por qué nuestro negocio convierte como convierte, qué fricciones existen y qué vamos a hacer al respecto. Por eso, buscamos a alguien que lidere la conversación sobre propuesta de valor, confianza, pricing, marca y experiencia omnicanal. Serás la persona owner de la conversión en Octopus Spain: desde la primera impresión de un anuncio hasta la venta, la instalación y el cross-sell posterior. 🤝 Trabajarás mano a mano con Marketing, Producto, Pricing, Ventas, Operaciones y equipos internacionales, reportando directamente al Growth Manager. Tu misión será clara: hacer que cada interacción con Octopus tenga más sentido, más coherencia y más impacto en negocio. 🐙 Lo llamamos el ‘Efecto Octopus’ Trabajar en Octopus Energy significa formar parte de una compañía donde la cultura importa de verdad. 💜 Apostamos por la autonomía y la responsabilidad real. 🚀 Valoramos la honestidad radical y el aprendizaje continuo. 🌱 Creemos en la tecnología como palanca de impacto. 🧠 Queremos personas curiosas, humildes y con ambición por hacer las cosas mejor cada día 🌍 Revolucionamos la energía para cambiar el mundo En Octopus Energy, no somos una eléctrica tradicional.
